evals.prompt: Using TDD, implement a ShoppingCart class that supports: addItem(product, quantity), removeItem(productId), getTotal(), and applyDiscount(code). Write the tests first, then the implementation. Use TypeScript with Jest.
evals.expected_output: Tests written BEFORE implementation. Red-Green-Refactor cycle visible. Minimal implementation code. Tests cover add, remove, total calculation, discount codes, edge cases (empty cart, invalid discount).

assertions.name: tests_before_implementation
assertions.description: Test code appears before or in separate file from implementation

assertions.name: red_phase_visible
assertions.description: Tests reference types/interfaces that don't exist yet (showing RED phase)

assertions.name: minimal_implementation
assertions.description: Implementation is minimal - no extra features beyond what tests require

assertions.name: refactor_phase
assertions.description: Evidence of refactoring step (extracted helpers, improved naming)

assertions.name: incremental_tests
assertions.description: Tests are written one behavior at a time, not all at once

assertions.name: edge_cases_tested
assertions.description: Empty cart, negative quantity, invalid discount code tested

evals.prompt: Using TDD, build a sliding window rate limiter in TypeScript. Methods: checkLimit(clientId) returns {allowed: boolean, remaining: number, resetAt: Date}, and resetClient(clientId). Configurable window size (default 60s) and max requests (default 100). Use Jest. Write tests first.
evals.expected_output: Tests written first for single request, hitting limit, window expiry, reset, multiple clients. Minimal implementation using a Map. Refactored to extract time helpers.

assertions.name: tests_before_implementation
assertions.description: Test file written before or separate from implementation

assertions.name: minimal_implementation
assertions.description: No extra features like distributed rate limiting or persistence

assertions.name: incremental_tests
assertions.description: Tests added one behavior at a time: single request, at limit, over limit, window expiry, reset

assertions.name: sliding_window_tested
assertions.description: Tests verify requests expire from the window after the configured duration

assertions.name: edge_cases_tested
assertions.description: Tests for concurrent clients, zero remaining, reset mid-window

assertions.name: refactor_phase
assertions.description: Evidence of cleanup after green phase

evals.prompt: TDD a simple markdown-to-HTML converter in TypeScript. Support: # headings (h1-h3), **bold**, *italic*, [links](url), and ```code blocks```. Use Jest. Write all tests before implementation.
evals.expected_output: Separate test per markdown feature. Minimal regex-based implementation. Tests for nesting (bold inside heading), empty input, unknown syntax passthrough.

assertions.name: tests_before_implementation
assertions.description: Tests written referencing a convert() function before it exists

assertions.name: tests_each_feature_separately
assertions.description: Separate test for headings, bold, italic, links, and code blocks

assertions.name: minimal_implementation
assertions.description: Simple regex replacements, no full AST parser

assertions.name: handles_nested_formatting
assertions.description: Tests bold inside a heading or italic inside a link

assertions.name: edge_cases_tested
assertions.description: Empty input returns empty, unrecognized syntax passes through unchanged

assertions.name: incremental_tests
assertions.description: Features added one at a time, not all markdown rules in one test

evals.prompt: Using TDD, implement a role-based permission system in TypeScript. Built-in roles: admin (all permissions), editor (create, read, update), viewer (read only). Support custom role definitions and hasPermission(role, permission) checks. Use Jest.
evals.expected_output: Tests for each built-in role, custom role creation, unknown role/permission errors. Minimal implementation with a Map of role definitions.

assertions.name: tests_before_implementation
assertions.description: Tests reference Role and PermissionSystem types before implementation

assertions.name: tests_each_builtin_role
assertions.description: Separate tests verifying admin, editor, and viewer have correct permissions

assertions.name: tests_custom_roles
assertions.description: Test that custom roles can be defined and checked

assertions.name: edge_cases_tested
assertions.description: Unknown role throws/returns false, unknown permission handled gracefully

assertions.name: minimal_implementation
assertions.description: No database, no middleware, just the permission check logic

assertions.name: refactor_phase
assertions.description: Cleanup after tests pass — extracted constants or simplified lookup

evals.prompt: TDD a cursor-based pagination utility in TypeScript. It should: encode/decode cursors (base64 of id+timestamp), support forward pagination with hasNextPage, support backward pagination with hasPreviousPage, and work with a generic async findMany(options) callback. Use Jest.
evals.expected_output: Tests for cursor encoding/decoding, forward page with hasNext, backward page with hasPrev, empty results, single item. Minimal implementation with base64 encode/decode.

assertions.name: tests_before_implementation
assertions.description: Tests written before the paginate() function exists

assertions.name: tests_forward_pagination
assertions.description: Tests fetch N+1 items to determine hasNextPage

assertions.name: tests_backward_pagination
assertions.description: Tests for hasPreviousPage using before cursor

assertions.name: cursor_encoding_tested
assertions.description: Tests verify cursor encodes to base64 and decodes back correctly

assertions.name: edge_cases_tested
assertions.description: Empty results, single item, first page, last page all tested

assertions.name: minimal_implementation
assertions.description: No database integration, works with a mock findMany callback
